# jdstemmler.dev

> Personal technical blog by Jayson Stemmler, a data scientist and homelabber.
> Write-ups of specific, hard-won fixes for homelab, self-hosting, and Claude
> Code problems. Every debugging post opens with the wrong hypothesis first,
> then the actual cause; each carries a SYMPTOM/CAUSE pair. No command output,
> version numbers, or benchmark figures are ever invented — if it's shown, it
> was actually run.

Notes for readers of any kind: pages are static HTML with zero client-side
JavaScript. IP addresses in posts are RFC 5737 documentation placeholders, and
network names are pseudonyms — described behavior is real, identifiers are not.

## Posts

- [My cameras were eating the 2.4GHz band](https://jdstemmler.dev/posts/cameras-eating-the-2-4ghz-band/):
  Nest Hubs kept dropping off WiFi; the cause was UniFi cameras streaming
  143 GB/day over 2.4GHz, saturating channel 1 at 95% airtime. Diagnosis via
  CEF disconnect logs and per-radio airtime stats; fixed with a 5GHz-only SSID
  on the same VLAN.
- [All posts](https://jdstemmler.dev/posts/): reverse-chronological index with
  symptom/cause previews.
- [RSS feed](https://jdstemmler.dev/rss.xml): full index; item summaries are
  the SYMPTOM/CAUSE pairs.

## Projects

- [onwrist.watch](https://jdstemmler.dev/projects/onwrist-watch/): a
  self-hosted, multi-tenant watch-collection tracker (SvelteKit + Postgres),
  designed as a single-user app and built overnight by an unattended Claude
  orchestrator, then rebuilt multi-tenant.
- [jdstemmler.dev](https://jdstemmler.dev/projects/jdstemmler-dev/): this site
  as a project — the content model, the Claude Code tooling that enforces the
  site's own honesty rules, and the build story.

## About

- [About](https://jdstemmler.dev/about/): who runs this site and where else to
  find their work.
